title: Cairn CASE.json manifest (cairn-case/1.0)
description: The engine-owned structural declaration a case bundle makes about itself: which apparently-independent lines actually launder one upstream (laundered_set + shared_upstream), and which pair is genuinely combinable (contrast_pair + contrast_expected). `cairn cases verify` runs these declarations against a record store; a case that stops refusing, or whose declared upstream is not the one the DAG walk collectively finds, fails. This is the case half of the versioned engine<->case interface (the record half is cairn.v0.schema.json). Version this manifest by bumping the default case_spec_version minor; the $id carries only the MAJOR (v1) and changes only on a breaking bump (v1->v2) to how the engine reads the manifest.

case_spec_version.description: The cairn-case manifest contract version this file targets -- the version component of the bundle's engine.pin `case_spec` (e.g. engine.pin case_spec 'cairn-case/1.0' => case_spec_version '1.0'). Optional for backward compatibility: absent means '1.0' (the seven grounded bundles predate the field and stay byte-identical). New case repos SHOULD declare it explicitly.

records.description: Optional ordered list of this bundle's record filenames (relative to records/), giving the record-insertion order that fixes the aggregate INDEX.json line order. REQUIRED for a self-contained (records/-shipping) bundle to reassemble byte-identically WITHOUT running build.py, since a filesystem sort is alphabetical and the golden order is not. In-tree dual-homed bundles omit it (their order comes from build.py); a migrated self-contained mirror declares it. See CASE-REPO-SPEC.md §2.

title.description: Human-readable name of the worked example.

crux.description: The single decision the case turns on, phrased as a question. Must match the `crux` of the declared battery (tests/test_cases.py::test_battery_declares_the_case_crux) when a battery is present.

shared_upstream.description: The one upstream every line in laundered_set actually descends from. A record slug (resolved via the store's filename-stem alias) OR a foreign identifier (doi:/urn:/... for import-path cases). `cairn cases verify` asserts this id is in the COLLECTIVE shared-upstream set the DAG walk finds over laundered_set -- shared by ALL lines, not merely some pair. May be reached only transitively (e.g. the eggs cohort backbone is two hops up).

shared_upstream_kind.description: Prose describing WHAT kind of thing the shared upstream is (one paper / one author collective / one dataset / one fabricated registry ...). Descriptive only; not read by the engine.

laundered_set.description: The apparently-independent lines that must collectively REFUSE-TO-COMBINE because they share `shared_upstream`. Slugs (or ids). This is the trap the case exists to spring.

contrast_pair.description: Optional genuinely-independent (or genuinely-entangled) pair, present to prove the engine is not a refuse-everything oracle. Its verdict must equal contrast_expected. Exactly two lines.

contrast_expected.description: The verdict contrast_pair must land on. Defaults to COMBINABLE when contrast_pair is present and this is absent (cairn/cases.py). SHOULD be present whenever contrast_pair is.

battery.description: Optional repo-relative path to the probe battery (assessment/probes.json shape) whose `crux` must match this manifest's. Vetted, span-grounded cases carry one; import-path cases (surgisphere shape) may omit it.

exercises.description: Optional prose: the specific structural pattern this case exercises that others do not (curator's note; not read by the engine).

import_note.description: Optional prose flagging that the case's records are minted via `cairn import` from cited identifiers (low rung, no span grounding) rather than first-party span-vetted sources.

punchline.description: One-paragraph plain-English statement of what the case demonstrates -- the sentence a reader keeps.
